subject: [PATCH 0/4] arm64/mm: contpte-sized exec folios for 16K and 64K pages

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Identified a double-put bug in fuse_try_move_page() where the folio refcount drops to zero prematurely. Suggested adding an extra folio_get() before replace_page_cache_folio() to maintain the reference.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch series addresses issues with contpte-sized exec folios on arm64, specifically for 16K and 64K pages. It fixes the calculation of optimal contpte order in exec_folio_order() and modifies the mmap_miss heuristic to enable readahead for executable memory. Additionally, it ensures that virtual addresses are aligned to CONT_PTE_SIZE for contpte mapping.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Raised questions about the memory pressure implications of the proposed changes, suggesting that they might not be worse than existing x86 behavior. Also mentioned an alternative approach using dynamic linker patches.

2026-03-13.patch_summary: The patch series proposes to introduce contpt-sized exec folios for 16K and 64K pages on arm64, addressing memory pressure concerns.
